# BookNook — Agent Instructions

> A **read-only projection of this project's spine Constitution** (`docs/spec/specification.md`). It tells any AI
> agent the project's non-negotiables and where the truth lives. It is **not** hand-maintained — edit the spine and
> regenerate. (This is the *per-project* AGENTS.md, distinct from the Fullstack Director framework's own AGENTS.md.)
> Consumed natively by Codex CLI; Claude Code imports it via `CLAUDE.md` line 1 (`@AGENTS.md`) and Gemini CLI via
> `GEMINI.md` (`@./AGENTS.md`) — the bridges 00-discovery creates when absent.

## Constitution (the project's non-negotiables)

<!-- verbatim from docs/spec/specification.md § Constitution -->
1. _Personal-first library. Every bookmark belongs to exactly one owner's library; sharing bookmarks between users
   is out of scope for v1._
2. _Tag over folder. Organization happens by tag, never by nested folders._
3. _Capture from any device. Saving a bookmark is a single action available from any device, phone included._
4. _Offline-available. Once saved, a bookmark's title and URL are readable offline; sync resumes automatically when
   the device is back online._

## Where the truth lives

- **Requirements = declaration-truth:** `docs/spec/` — the spine. `specification.md` is the index (Constitution +
  REQ registry); detailed requirements sit in `capabilities/<domain>.md`; declared design intent and architecture
  mandates in `design-intent.md` / `architecture-constraints.md`.
- Everything else (`docs/design/`, `docs/architecture/`, `src/`, …) **references** requirements by `REQ-NNN` — it
  never restates their text.
